mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, which forces equity markets to price in lower earnings growth over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer spending without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate output capacity and improving profit margins, which drives short-term equity momentum over the next week during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during easing periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and dividend payouts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% forces markets to anticipate immediate central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized economic expansion across labor and construction sectors, supporting sustained corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts signals deteriorating consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Housing declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal produces positive returns in >60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases.
